perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Research Quantities" means, in the case of nanomaterials and coated nanomaterials, quantities purchased and distributed in individual orders of 54 kilograms or less, and, in the case of dispersions of nanomaterials, quantities purchased and distributed in individual orders of 60 kilograms or less.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Research Quantities" means, in the case of nanomaterials and coated nanomaterials, quantities purchased and distributed in individual orders of 54 kilograms or less, and, in the case of dispersions of nanomaterials, quantities purchased and distributed in individual orders of 60 kilograms or less. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, ALFA AESAR may request and NTC may approve, in their sole discretion, orders exceeding these quantities to still be considered 'Research Quantities'.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a sentence allowing for 'Research Quantities' to exceed the defined limits at the discretion of both parties. This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original definition by making it subjective and conditional, which impacts the enforcement of the agreement and the rights and obligations of both parties regarding pricing and exclusivity for 'Research Quantities'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NTC hereby grants to ALFA AESAR, and its subsidiaries and affiliates, the exclusive right to market, sell and distribute Research Quantities of the Products within the Territory, ALFA AESAR hereby accepts such right and agrees to use its reasonable efforts to promote the marketing, sale and distribution of Research Quantities of the Products throughout the Territory in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement using normal and standard practices. ALFA AESAR shall have the right, in its sole discretion, to sell Products outside of the Territory. ALFA AESAR agrees that, so long as NTC provides ALFA AESAR with Products for ALFA AESAR' S marketing, sale and distribution of Research Quantities of the Products within the Territory, ALFA AESAR shall purchase all its requirements of the Products (including nanomaterials with physical or chemical properties substantially the same as the Products) exclusively from NTC during the term of this Agreement, ALFA AESAR shall: i) buy the Products in quantities listed in Schedule A and repackage into the research sample quantities as it deems reasonable and appropriate for distribution and sale on a worldwide basis at such resale prices it shall determine from time to time, provided that any such repackaging shall comply with applicable safety laws and regulations; ii) attempt to keep reasonably sufficient stocks of the Products to meet customer orders and to store such stocks in such conditions as NTC may recommend from time to time to prevent deterioration or damage.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Research Quantities' with specific weight limits. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing quantities exceeding these limits to still be considered 'Research Quantities' at the discretion of both parties. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants ALFA AESAR exclusive rights to market, sell, and distribute 'Research Quantities' within a defined territory. The changed text makes the definition of 'Research Quantities' subjective and conditional, undermining the exclusivity granted to ALFA AESAR and creating uncertainty about the scope of their rights, as NTC could potentially approve larger quantities for other distributors.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 NTC may discontinue offering any Product for any reason by providing six (6) months advance written notice to ALFA AESAR, provided however, NTC shall continue supplying ALFA AESAR with such discontinued Product until the expiration of such six (6) month notice period. Notwithstanding the foregoing, NTC may discontinue any Product immediately upon written notice to ALFA AESAR if such discontinuation is due to any actual or alleged Product defect which may actually or allegedly cause damage to person, property rights or property, With respect to ALFA AESAR's existing inventory of discontinued Product, ALFA AESAR may, its sole discretion, return such Product at any time to NTC for full credit or refund.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 NTC may discontinue offering any Product for any reason by providing six (6) months advance written notice to ALFA AESAR, provided however, NTC shall not be obligated to continue supplying ALFA AESAR with such discontinued Product after providing notice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, NTC may discontinue any Product immediately upon written notice to ALFA AESAR if such discontinuation is due to any actual or alleged Product defect which may actually or allegedly cause damage to person, property rights or property, With respect to ALFA AESAR's existing inventory of discontinued Product, ALFA AESAR may, its sole discretion, return such Product at any time to NTC for full credit or refund.
perturbation.explanation: This modification states that NTC is not obligated to continue supplying discontinued products after the notice period. This directly contradicts the original clause which explicitly stated NTC 'shall continue supplying' during the six-month notice period. This contradiction makes it unclear what ALFA AESAR is entitled to and creates uncertainty for enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NTC may discontinue offering any Product for any reason by providing six (6) months advance written notice to ALFA AESAR, provided however, NTC shall not be obligated to continue supplying ALFA AESAR with such discontinued Product after providing notice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, NTC may discontinue any Product immediately upon written notice to ALFA AESAR if such discontinuation is due to any actual or alleged Product defect which may actually or allegedly cause damage to person, property rights or property, With respect to ALFA AESAR's existing inventory of discontinued Product, ALFA AESAR may, its sole discretion, return such Product at any time to NTC for full credit or refund.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5.2 states that NTC is *not obligated* to continue supplying ALFA AESAR with discontinued product after providing notice. This directly contradicts the original version of the same section, which mandated NTC *shall continue supplying* during the six-month notice period. Since the contradicted text is the changed text itself, this creates a direct and undeniable in-text contradiction regarding NTC's obligation to supply discontinued products during the notice period, thus creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five (5) years from the date first set forth above and shall thereafter automatically renew for additional two (2) year terms unless a party provides the other party with notice of non-renewal no less than 6 months prior to the expiration of the initial term or any renewal term unless earlier terminated as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five (5) years from the date first set forth above and shall thereafter automatically renew for additional two (2) year terms unless a party provides the other party with notice of non-renewal no less than 6 months prior to the expiration of the initial term or any renewal term unless earlier terminated as follows. However, the agreement may not renew despite there being no notice of non-renewal, according to the discretion of NTC.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence provides NTC the discretion to not renew the contract even if no non-renewal notice was provided, thus contradicting the prior sentence, which dictates an 'automatic renewal' if no notice is provided. This creates ambiguity about the renewal conditions and provides an out for NTC, even if ALFA AESAR expects renewal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five (5) years from the date first set forth above and shall thereafter automatically renew for additional two (2) year terms unless a party provides the other party with notice of non-renewal no less than 6 months prior to the expiration of the initial term or any renewal term unless earlier terminated as follows. However, the agreement may not renew despite there being no notice of non-renewal, according to the discretion of NTC.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement automatically renews unless a non-renewal notice is provided. The changed text introduces an exception where NTC can choose not to renew the agreement even if no notice is given, directly contradicting the automatic renewal clause. This creates a strong inconsistency regarding the renewal terms, making the agreement unenforceable. This aligns with Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction: Structural flaws also cause **in-text contradictions** when a clause is placed in **multiple locations with conflicting implications**.
